ogTitle: Trawool vs Traynors Lagoon: Climate Safety

heading: Trawool vs Traynors Lagoon — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Trawool, Victoria and Traynors Lagoon, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Trawool has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Traynors Lagoon (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Trawool or Traynors Lagoon safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Trawool has a flood risk score of 22/100, while Traynors Lagoon scores 58/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Trawool scores 59/100 for bushfire risk. Traynors Lagoon scores 64/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Trawool or Traynors Lagoon?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Trawool has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.03% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
